If anyone says, "It feels like the old game from the X franchise, really reminds me of games like Y", that is a red flag. It is a nostalgia bait tactic used to trick players into feeling that there is a connection to something they hold dear. It's used when the studio knows the games are obviously not as bad as their last title, but not even close to the standard quality of the whole franchise. Best example of this in effect in practice is some of EAs ' disinformation efforts leading up to Battlefield 6.

The ultimate SAO experience needs character continuity!

The inclusion of the new one-life mode in Sword Art Online: Echoes of Aincrad is an absolute game-changer. For the first time in a video game, I will be able to experience the true high-stakes immersion of the original anime. Getting so deeply attached to a unique avatar while knowing that one single mistake will mean permanent data deletion will bring an unmatched level of consistency to the lore and the universe.

However, to truly respect this incredible emotional investment, Bandai Namco needs to hear us: it is absolutely vital that we get the opportunity to transfer our characters and their legacy into Vol. 2.

Please, let us keep our survivors’ stories alive in Vol. 2!

It was supposed to be the game of the century, and instead it's yet another fan game, graphically mediocre, still have to be seen in 2026? Damn, there are definitely better games out there on smartphones, not to mention that it's being pushed by an anime to create hype, considering that a 100-story Tower was downplayed to just 2 floors... Gentlemen, there are games that in 10 years have churned out the unthinkable, here I wonder what went wrong... Budget? Because if that's all it takes, we might as well wait longer for a complete product, not a product with this result... I used to be a fan who would have defended this saga tooth and nail, but not anymore. Now it's just another game made just to make money... Years ago I could even agree with him, there wasn't the technology to achieve certain results, but not today, the rest are just excuses.  I don't care about multiplayer or anything like that, I just wanted to finally see a game that honored what we knew about the series so far. But it's clear that there's no real interest behind it. Greetings from a highly disappointed fan.

As for the aircraft, AC7 features 48 planes, including those from the DLC. Familiar aircraft from previous installments are joined by newcomers. For the F-5J, MiG-31B, and Su-35S, this marks their debut in the Strangereal universe. There is also new weaponry such as a pulsed laser, a tactical laser system, an electromagnetic cannon, and conventional weapons like the AGM-158B JAASM-ER, Taurus KEPD 350, CH-59MK2, Brimstone 2, APKWS II, and eight AIM-120C-7 air-to-air missiles. The Lockheed F-104C Starfighter—a fighter-bomber version of Clarence “Kelly” Johnson, making it the oldest fighter in the game and one of the oldest in the series’ history; modernized versions of the A-10 and Gripen in the form of the Fairchild Republic A-10C Thunderbolt II and the rising star Saab JAS 39E Gripen; and the Sukhoi Su-30M2 “Flanker-G” from the Komsomolsk line and the Su-30SM “Flanker-H” from the Irkutsk line, the production version of the Lockheed Martin F-15C Lightning II, and the new fictional EASA/NOGI X-02S Strike Wyvern fighter—a modernized version of the X-02 Wyvern. The first aircraft in the DLC was the McDonnell Douglas F-4E Phantom II, along with three iconic skins from three installments of the series on PS2. As part of the season pass, three fighters from the ADF series were introduced: the SBMF ADFX-01 Morgan, the NOGI ADF-01 FALKEN, and the debuting NOGI/EASA ADF-11F Raven. The original fighters also returned in the form of the XFA-27, the Albastru-Electrice CFA-44 Nosferatu “Fandance,” and the Taiga ASF-X Shinden II - making its debut in Strangereal. Skins from Ace Combat Zero: The Belkan War have also been added, featuring a revolutionary take on the Belkan Air Force’s markings, inspired by the German Luftwaffe as well as the Italian and Spanish Air Forces. Prototype aircraft have also returned, including the General Dynamics F-16XL, McDonnell Douglas F-15 S/MTD, and Lockheed Martin FB-22 Strike Raptor. The DLC featuring upgraded fighters turned out to be a real surprise. The MiG-35D (UB) “Fulcrum-F,” known from AC Infinity, was joined by the Mitsubishi F-2A Super Kai and the Boeing F/A-18F Super Hornet Block III. Finally, the dream of both Ace Combat fans and Project Aces itself has come true: a set of aircraft from the movie “Top Gun: Maverick,” including the Skunk Works-developed DarkStar hypersonic aircraft (though it had worse performance than in Microsoft Flight Simulator due to map limitations in AC7), production and movie versions of the Grumman F-14A Tomcat and Boeing F/A-18E Super Hornet (the latter features a detachable fuel tank under the fuselage), both returning after a long absence, as well as the fifth-generation enemy fighter, the Sukhoi Su-57 “Felon.” Although fans of both series complained about the absence of the MiG-28, i.e., the Northrop F-5E Tiger II. The game’s flagship fighter is once again the Lockheed Martin F-22A Raptor in the Block 40 version.

Ace Combat 7: Skies Unknown will celebrate its 5th anniversary in 2024, and the Ace Combat series will celebrate its 30th anniversary in 2025.

The first aircraft in the DLC was the McDonnell Douglas F-4E Phantom II, along with three iconic skins from three installments of the series on PS2. As part of the season pass, three fighters from the ADF series were introduced: the SBMF ADFX-01 Morgan, the NOGI ADF-01 FALKEN, and the debuting NOGI/EASA ADF-11F Raven. The original fighters also returned in the form of the XFA-27, the Albastru-Electrice CFA-44 Nosferatu “Fandance,” and the Taiga ASF-X Shinden II - making its debut in Strangereal. Skins from Ace Combat Zero: The Belkan War have also been added, featuring a revolutionary take on the Belkan Air Force’s markings, inspired by the German Luftwaffe as well as the Italian and Spanish Air Forces. Prototype aircraft have also returned, including the General Dynamics F-16XL, McDonnell Douglas F-15 S/MTD, and Lockheed Martin FB-22 Strike Raptor. The DLC featuring upgraded fighters turned out to be a real surprise. The MiG-35D (UB) “Fulcrum-F,” known from AC Infinity, was joined by the Mitsubishi F-2A Super Kai and the Boeing F/A-18F Super Hornet Block III. Finally, the dream of both Ace Combat fans and Project Aces itself has come true: a set of aircraft from the movie “Top Gun: Maverick,” including the Skunk Works-developed DarkStar hypersonic aircraft (though it had worse performance than in Microsoft Flight Simulator due to map limitations in AC7), production and movie versions of the Grumman F-14A Tomcat and Boeing F/A-18E Super Hornet (the latter features a detachable fuel tank under the fuselage), both returning after a long absence, as well as the fifth-generation enemy fighter, the Sukhoi Su-57 “Felon.” Although fans of both series complained about the absence of the MiG-28, i.e., the Northrop F-5E Tiger II. The game’s flagship fighter is once again the Lockheed Martin F-22A Raptor in the Block 40 version.

Ace Combat 7: Skies Unknown will celebrate its 5th anniversary in 2024, and the Ace Combat series will celebrate its 30th anniversary in 2025.

You really need to add the options for both SMOOTH TURNING and the removal of the 'hood' vignette.

Snap-turn only is just not acceptable to most seasoned VR players, and neither is a forced vignette.

I get it. The 'hood' is part of the Little Nightmares canon, and it's kinda cute to experience it in VR, but the simple fact is that the vast majority of VR gamers HATE having our field of view forcibly restricted.

Yes, some gamers find these comfort options necessary, so it's great to have them available, but they need to be just that: OPTIONS!

Bandai needs to understand what Capcom, Ubisoft, Warner, Cd Projekt Red, Blizzard, Microsoft, Sony, and many others have already understood... Localizing games with voice acting in as many languages ​​as possible is key to building player loyalty and bringing them closer. If you want your game to sell at full price on day one, you need this differentiator too. Players want to know they'll enjoy what the developers created by looking at the screen and playing, not having to read text subtitles while things are happening. Ace Combat Infinity - the final game in the series for seventh-generation consoles, is also unusual in that it is a free-to-play title for the PlayStation 3, featuring only eight story missions with a cliffhanger ending and focusing on multiplayer aerial combat. Although it received mixed reviews, the game, which debuted in May 2014, was quite successful, leading to the creation of Ace Combat 7: Skies Unknown. At the end of March 2018, the game was finally shut down. Ace Combat Infinity largely draws inspiration from Ace Combat 04: Shattered Skies and is a hybrid of our world and Strangereal, as well as the quintessence of the series’ first 20 years. The graphics engine developed by Project Aces and used in AC6 and Assault Horizon began to show the first signs of aging. For a new game on next-gen consoles and with ever-increasing PC requirements, a revolution was decided upon, but more on that with AC7. The remix of “Blue Skies” featuring Stephanie Cooke is one of the best tracks in the Ace Combat series, and even in the entire history of video games. The game includes many references to previous installments in the series.

In the 1990s, the asteroid 1986VG1 Ulysses was discovered; it had previously orbited Jupiter. Although humanity prevented a catastrophe on the scale of mass extinction in July 1999 thanks to the Stonehenge interceptor network, numerous fragments caused extensive damage, especially on the Eurasian continent, and Japan decided to relocate its capital from Tokyo. The economy collapsed, and a wave of refugees emerged. Due to shifts in national budgets toward global reconstruction, the defense budgets of many countries suffered. Reconstruction in the early 21st century consumed 58% of the global budget ($1.845 trillion USD), while only 3% was spent on defense. Recognizing the collapse of global security, the UN underwent a reorganization and established its own armed forces independent of member states, comprising ground forces, a navy, and an air force. Meanwhile, the Werhner and Noah Enterprises began investing in the revitalization of the defense industry and other sectors of the economy. After acquiring aircraft designs and other components in Special Economic Zones (such as Iyuli in Russia), fully automated factories were built to produce fighter jets of every generation, from the MiG-21bis and F-5E to advanced fighters like the F-35 and futuristic aircraft. Due to the mass production of pilots, many of whom had extensive combat experience, became mercenaries. A boom erupted in the PMC market. Private military companies operating under the auspices of the UN operate within the framework of the United Nations Independent Commando Services (UNICS). One such company is Arrows Air Defense and Security, where a new recruit named “Reaper” joined in 2019. Twenty years after the tragedy on the Eurasian continent, armed uprisings broke out in economic zones due to the mistreatment of refugees. In May, an uprising broke out led by the Sons of Troy, a multinational network of armed terrorist groups. The UN dispatched the “Bone Arrow” Squadron, consisting of “Reaper,” the veteran “Viper,” “Omega,” and “Bronco.” Together with UN forces and the Japan Air Self-Defense Force, they achieved many victories, but during Operation Overdrive on August 10, 2019, UN forces suffered a Pyrrhic victory in Tokyo. No one knew that behind the Sons of Troy’s actions stood Kacper Cohen, head of the weapons division at the Werhner and Noah corporation, who was disillusioned with the UN’s stance on the mistreatment of refugees. After being fired from the company, together with several countries and terrorist organizations, he established the USEA Federation—a megastate the size of the Mongol Empire, spanning territory from China to Central Europe (unfortunately, Poland also fell victim to the Federation) - with the goal of dismantling the UN. In response, the UN Military Staff Committee established Task Force 118, which includes, among others, the “Bone Arrow” and “Ridgebacks” squadrons—an elite UNAF squadron. As part of Operation “Internal Liberation,” the goal was set to defeat the USEA Federation and save Paris, Tokyo, and Dubai from being captured by Federation forces. An additional adversary turned out to be the mysterious “Butterfly Master,” the flying QFA-44 Carmilla, for whom the war was nothing more than a game.

As for aircraft, the planes featured in Assault Horizon (with the exception of the AC-130U Spooky and helicopters) have been joined by many aircraft from previous installments, such as the F-4G, F-14A, F-14B, F-16XL, EA-18G, MiG-1.44, Su-27, F-5E, F-20A, X-29, F-15SE, XFA-27, X-02, XFA-33 Fenrir, ADFX-01 Morgan, ADF-01 FALKEN, R-101 Delphinius #1, A-6E, EA-6B, J35J, X-49 Night Raven, and FB-22. New aircraft have also been added, including the F-35A, F-15J, MiG-35D and Su-24M. Infinity also introduced the ADA-01B ADLER - a strike variant of the ADF-01 - and the Mitsubishi ATD-0, an armed version of the X-2 Shinshin technology demonstrator. This is the result of a collaboration between Project Aces and the Research and Development Technical Institute of the Ministry of Defense of the Empire of Japan. The biplane SKY KID has also been introduced, along with the debut of the Ace Combat mascot Nugget, as well as legendary World War II piston-engine fighters such as the Mitsubishi A6M5 Zero, Messerschmitt Bf 109 G-10, Supermarine Spitfire Mk.IXe, and Lockheed P-38L Lightning, also featuring skins of legendary aces. Counting all aircraft (standard, special, with various skins, and those from collaborations with “Aera 88” and the IDOLM@STER series), there are 364 in total. Will Ace Combat 8: Wings of Theve come close to this number, or will it be more like Ace Combat 5: The Unsung War with 53 aircraft?

The game’s flagship fighter is once again the Lockheed Martin F-22A Raptor.

Ace Combat Infinity celebrates its 10th anniversary in 2024, and the Ace Combat series marks its 30th anniversary in 2025.

Ace Combat Infinity largely draws inspiration from Ace Combat 04: Shattered Skies and is a hybrid of our world and Strangereal, as well as the quintessence of the series’ first 20 years. The graphics engine developed by Project Aces and used in AC6 and Assault Horizon began to show the first signs of aging. For a new game on next-gen consoles and with ever-increasing PC requirements, a revolution was decided upon, but more on that with AC7. The remix of “Blue Skies” featuring Stephanie Cooke is one of the best tracks in the Ace Combat series, and even in the entire history of video games. The game includes many references to previous installments in the series.

In the 1990s, the asteroid 1986VG1 Ulysses was discovered; it had previously orbited Jupiter. Although humanity prevented a catastrophe on the scale of mass extinction in July 1999 thanks to the Stonehenge interceptor network, numerous fragments caused extensive damage, especially on the Eurasian continent, and Japan decided to relocate its capital from Tokyo. The economy collapsed, and a wave of refugees emerged. Due to shifts in national budgets toward global reconstruction, the defense budgets of many countries suffered. Reconstruction in the early 21st century consumed 58% of the global budget ($1.845 trillion USD), while only 3% was spent on defense. Recognizing the collapse of global security, the UN underwent a reorganization and established its own armed forces independent of member states, comprising ground forces, a navy, and an air force. Meanwhile, the Werhner and Noah Enterprises began investing in the revitalization of the defense industry and other sectors of the economy. After acquiring aircraft designs and other components in Special Economic Zones (such as Iyuli in Russia), fully automated factories were built to produce fighter jets of every generation, from the MiG-21bis and F-5E to advanced fighters like the F-35 and futuristic aircraft. Due to the mass production of pilots, many of whom had extensive combat experience, became mercenaries. A boom erupted in the PMC market. Private military companies operating under the auspices of the UN operate within the framework of the United Nations Independent Commando Services (UNICS). One such company is Arrows Air Defense and Security, where a new recruit named “Reaper” joined in 2019. Twenty years after the tragedy on the Eurasian continent, armed uprisings broke out in economic zones due to the mistreatment of refugees. In May, an uprising broke out led by the Sons of Troy, a multinational network of armed terrorist groups. The UN dispatched the “Bone Arrow” Squadron, consisting of “Reaper,” the veteran “Viper,” “Omega,” and “Bronco.” Together with UN forces and the Japan Air Self-Defense Force, they achieved many victories, but during Operation Overdrive on August 10, 2019, UN forces suffered a Pyrrhic victory in Tokyo. No one knew that behind the Sons of Troy’s actions stood Kacper Cohen, head of the weapons division at the Werhner and Noah corporation, who was disillusioned with the UN’s stance on the mistreatment of refugees. After being fired from the company, together with several countries and terrorist organizations, he established the USEA Federation—a megastate the size of the Mongol Empire, spanning territory from China to Central Europe (unfortunately, Poland also fell victim to the Federation) - with the goal of dismantling the UN. In response, the UN Military Staff Committee established Task Force 118, which includes, among others, the “Bone Arrow” and “Ridgebacks” squadrons—an elite UNAF squadron. As part of Operation “Internal Liberation,” the goal was set to defeat the USEA Federation and save Paris, Tokyo, and Dubai from being captured by Federation forces. An additional adversary turned out to be the mysterious “Butterfly Master,” the flying QFA-44 Carmilla, for whom the war was nothing more than a game.

As for aircraft, the planes featured in Assault Horizon (with the exception of the AC-130U Spooky and helicopters) have been joined by many aircraft from previous installments, such as the F-4G, F-14A, F-14B, F-16XL, EA-18G, MiG-1.44, Su-27, F-5E, F-20A, X-29, F-15SE, XFA-27, X-02, XFA-33 Fenrir, ADFX-01 Morgan, ADF-01 FALKEN, R-101 Delphinius #1, A-6E, EA-6B, J35J, X-49 Night Raven, and FB-22. New aircraft have also been added, including the F-35A, F-15J, MiG-35D and Su-24M. Infinity also introduced the ADA-01B ADLER - a strike variant of the ADF-01 - and the Mitsubishi ATD-0, an armed version of the X-2 Shinshin technology demonstrator. This is the result of a collaboration between Project Aces and the Research and Development Technical Institute of the Ministry of Defense of the Empire of Japan. The biplane SKY KID has also been introduced, along with the debut of the Ace Combat mascot Nugget, as well as legendary World War II piston-engine fighters such as the Mitsubishi A6M5 Zero, Messerschmitt Bf 109 G-10, Supermarine Spitfire Mk.IXe, and Lockheed P-38L Lightning, also featuring skins of legendary aces. Counting all aircraft (standard, special, with various skins, and those from collaborations with “Aera 88” and the IDOLM@STER series), there are 364 in total. Will Ace Combat 8: Wings of Theve come close to this number, or will it be more like Ace Combat 5: The Unsung War with 53 aircraft?

The game’s flagship fighter is once again the Lockheed Martin F-22A Raptor.

Ace Combat Infinity celebrates its 10th anniversary in 2024, and the Ace Combat series marks its 30th anniversary in 2025.

Thank You Project ACES and Bandai Namco ACES for using the Aircraft Carrier USS Hornet Museum (CVS-12) in Alameda, CA, USA, as a reference for Ace Combat 8 Wings of Theve for the Aircraft Carrier FCU Endurance. I am a big Ace Combat fan and when I heard the FCU Endurance reference point in Ace Combat 8 is based on the USS Hornet Museum, I am truly excited, both myself and my Mom are truly happy, and excited about this because both of us are born and raised in the City of Alameda. We both been onboard the USS Hornet Museum for many years and know the history.
----------Thank You Again to the team from Project ACES to use the USS Hornet Museum as a reference for the game. 😁😁😁😁

Also, The USS Hornet Museum is the home of Carrier Con.

I truly hope AC8 will have some sort of repeatable mode outside of multiplayer pvp at some point in its life cycle. Project Wingman's conquest mode was a great idea, if a little rough around the edges, and Nuclear Option's escalation mode has been a recent highlight to me in terms of what I'd like. I always replay these games every so often, they're much more than a one and done story to me, but I'd truly adore some sort of non-narrative PvE mode that I could play solo or with a couple friends. I truly hope such a thing is somewhere in mind with the Project Aces devs. "Online mode that's the biggest in the series" gives me a little hope, but with how vague that is, please just let it be more than pvp!

1) Functional instruments (Falken) and rear-view mirrors (SU-35/57) in the cockpit.

When will there be a remake of Ace Combat 3 in its Japanese version? had 52 missions, 5 endings, 23 aircraft, many futuristic, which were never seen again in other titles... (well, Ace Combat 7 and Infinity did release several as DLC). Decisions in missions could change the objectives and the story in subsequent battles, and the story was told like a visual novel. This work was done by Production I.G. (the studio behind Ghost in the Shell), and to this day, no game has been released with the same amount of content.

I'm from Brazil, the first Tales of game I played was the original Tales of Destiny on the PSX, of course, playing it on my PSP, and it was one of the best RPGs I've ever played. I got very attached to the characters and remember all their names, the pixel art, even though it's old, is very beautiful, along with the gorgeous artwork by Mutsumi Inomata, and the soundtrack is so memorable.😃 
I really ship Stanh and Rutee, and I'm happy they ended up together.😊 It's just sad that it happened off-screen. Was it too much to ask for an explicit kissing scene between them?😐 
Anyway, Tales of Destiny is undoubtedly one of the best RPGs on the PSX, and a great sequel to the franchise after Tales of Phantasia. I'm even playing the original SNES version, and it's just as good.🙃 
I already have Tales of Zestiria, Berseria, Symphonia, Vesperia, and Arise to play on PC, and I can also play Eternia. On my PSP, and now that I have a Nintendo Switch Lite I can also play Graces and Xillia or even the ones I already have in portable mode. I can't wait for the traditions of the remaining games to come out so I can play them too.😁 
I hope Tales of continues to be one of the best RPG franchises out there.❤️

Ace Combat 3D: Cross Rumble (known in the West as Ace Combat: Assault Horizon Legacy – a title that was highly confusing given the release of Assault Horizon at the time) – the second game in the series for the Nintendo console. In this case, the 3DS. Released in the West in late 2011 and in its home country of Japan in early 2012, this instalment is so far the only remake of a previous game in the series, in this case Ace Combat 2, though it also references the first Ace Combat. In 2015, the Plus version was released, utilising the new capabilities of the 3DS and interaction with amiibo figures. The graphics engine is derived from Joint Assault.

Following the Belkan War and the end of the Cold War, two superpowers - the Osea Federation and the Union of Yuktobanian Republics - grew in strength, which alarmed the nations of the Usea continent. These nations decided to form an alliance known as the Usean Allied Forces to counterbalance both powers. However, over time, the southern states decided to join forces with Osea, which did not please the states in the northern and western parts of the continent, who feared losing their rights to extract resources in the southern part of the continent and the arrival of Osea Defence Force soldiers. On 30 May 1997, the day the Sea of Spring Treaty was signed, radical militarists from conservative extremist factions, including a significant portion of the former UAF forces, organised and carried out a coup d’état across the entire continent, plunging Usea into civil war. The remnants of the loyal UAF forces evacuated to the Twinkle Islands. In order to defeat the coup forces, the UAF launched Operation Fighter's Honor, calling upon the mercenary 1st Special Tactical Fighter Squadron ‘Scarface’, led by ‘Phoenix’, for assistance. After defending the Twinkle Islands, from 20 March 1998 the ‘Scarface’ squadron began the long road to liberating the continent, fighting against squadrons such as ‘Lancer’, ‘Beast’, “Cocoon” and ‘Albireo’. On more than one occasion, the remaining units were rescued, and Saint Ark was even saved twice. It then came to light that the coup forces possessed a secret weapon: autonomous fighters equipped with Zone Of Endless (Z.O.E.) artificial intelligence, secretly supplied by Belka. In an act of desperation, on 18 September 1998, the coup forces intended to launch an intercontinental ballistic missile from the Fortress Intolerance in North Point towards Yuktobania in order to provoke a global conflict. ‘Phoenix’, using the XFA-27, defeated the ADF-01 FALKEN and prevented an even more dangerous conflict, thereby ending the war. ‘Scarface’’s combat route is the reverse of the ISAF’s combat route in Ace Combat 04: Shattered Skies.

As for the aircraft, most are the same as in Ace Combat 2. But there have been some changes. The YF-22 has been changed to the F-22A, the F-14A to the F-14D, and so on. The Mirage 2000D, MiG-1.44, MiG-21-93, Su-37, Su-47 and Su-57 (PAK FA) have also been introduced, as well as the F-35C, Gripen NG and F-15SE, and the piston-engine fighters Zero and Hellcat. Machines from the original, such as the Kfir C.7 and A-4 Skyhawk, were missing; the changes to the aircraft began to call into question the game’s canon, and the F-35C, Gripen NG, Su-47, F-15SE and Su-57 are anachronisms, as they did not actually exist in 1997–1998. The game’s flagship fighter is once again the Lockheed Martin F-22A Raptor from the EMD series. We can only hope that the F-22A “Scarface” skin, as well as the Z.O.E. skins, ‘Lancer’, ‘Beast’, “Cocoon” and ‘Albireo’ will return along with their markings in Ace Combat 8: Wings of Theve.

The 15th anniversary of Ace Combat: Assault Horizon Legacy in 2026/2027 and the 30th anniversary of the Ace Combat series in 2025.

It was 5 years between Berseria and Arise. Arise was announced 3 years after the release of Berseria. Now here we are 5 years after the release of Arise and 2.5 years after the release of it's DLC and not so much as an announcement of a new game. This is the largest gap the franchise has ever seen.

And the remasters have all been handled by different teams, the core Tales team is (hopefully) still working on something new.

SAO Baby!!!!

I haven't really done much with SAO since Ordinal Scale. But I loved the Aincrad arc for all its possibilities. How society within the death game functioned. The apparent discrimination against the Beta Testers. How younger players would handle it (talking like preteens). Griefers playing to screw with others... how would they react if they killed someone in what was supposed to be trolling? How that constant fear you could die would change you... and not just from other players or the game itself, but knowing your body could die any time, wondering if it's hooked to life support or just wasting away... There were so many possibilities it felt like the series glossed over. So I think it's worth diving back in, to see if it does something with all those concepts. And hopefully gives us a little more freedom in the story instead of making us retread Kirito's path.

Honestly, remasters are getting boring. Make remakes from scratch, like Final Fantasy VII, Resident Evil 2 and 4! Enough with the same old thing with increased resolution! Sequels or remakes from scratch! And the interesting thing in this case is that you made a REMASTER, and you're not going to give a paid or free upgrade to those who already have the original version. It's the height of blatant exploitation of people's money! You offer nothing new and want to sell it again! It's getting tiresome! I think, with a minimum of shame, you should ideally give a free upgrade, or at least a paid upgrade for a symbolic amount. I'm not going to support these predatory practices of this increasingly disrespectful industry. I won't buy the same game again and again! And I suggest that people start using their brains too! I just really want Pac Man World Rally 2005 beta prototype tracks and battle arenas. I'd prefer the original, but if it is remade as accurately as possible in a Pac Man World Rally Re-Pac, I'd still greatly appreciate it. Ever since the original game came out, I felt so betrayed because the final game's levels like Galactic Outpost, Arctic Iceberg, and Rolling Hills look no where near as good as the 2005 prototype's levels like Lunar Launch, Frozen Tundra, and Rolling Hills Raceway. All of the prototype levels were just MUCH more cool looking, visually, pleasing, and well designed, including actual track layout than the mockery of it we publicly got in 2006 when it released. I still LOVE Pac Man World Rally, don't get me wrong. It is one of my most favorite games of all time, but I am absolutely gutted that we didn't get the versions of the levels that I wanted since every track had much superior designs that went unused when they were originally created. Even the textures and lighting don't look right in the final, too fake.

Spooky's look and voice are no where near as threatening and cool they are in the original, however, I absolutely LOVE the return of Pac Man's voice from Pac Man World 3. Pac Man World 3 was the only Pac Man game that gave 100% perfect voices to every character, in my opinion, and I hope all of the original actors return doing the exact same voices and keep the same script unedited and only add scenes with new dialog, not change or take away any that already exist in the original. The script is iconic and perfect!
Woo!

Pac Man World Rally is one of my all-time favorite games too, but I 100% agree that I just want all of the 2005 beta prototype race tracks, battle arenas, and visual designs exactly as they were when they were initially created instead of the redesigns used in the 2006 and 2007 final game public release versions. The final game's designs, especially on the PSP due to limitations, are seriously inferior to the track layouts, hazards, models, and textures used in the 2005 prototype. The 2005 prototype had much more beautiful, cool, and thrilling colors, textures, road layouts, stronger slants to the track, a more realistic, darker, less reflective and cartoony lighting setting that looked just like Pac Man World 2 and was better for it. Every design aspect was better, like having the jumps in Lunar Launch unobscured space with tons of white stars on a black sky with the silver Pac Man Moon and orange nebula. Countless superior details like that in it. Can we either just have that publicly released or an exact one for one recreation of those levels for Pac Man World Rally Re-Pac?

Cool game. But the game has LESS active players that Fatal Bullet besides around big update days.

Amazing concept that just stopped working. There was very few balancing changes, they BUFFED Kirito despite him being top 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5 on plenty of bosses because you just press one button with him over and over and win. Same for Asuna. Yui never got good enough nerfs and makes certain bosses into "Yui simulator." Balancing aside, they threw away all joy for the game when "new content" was just reskins of old content, and the rewards became all T-shirts with nothing really that interesting. Launch day bosses had much better drops and a more fun experience. Only new bosses added was an OS boss and Fuscus (best one). Cool bosses like Fatal Scythe and Gabriel himself were reduced to mere Free roam slop bosses. Seriously, Gabriel, Fatal Scythe, hell even the Floor 100 boss would have worked amazing for coop or boss raid bosses and was such a missed opportunity.

Doesn't help the game also drifted into the realm of copy-paste. Cait Sith Alice was mostly unique but reused every attack and normal animation that base Alice had so it was like $7 for a skill switch. Fair. But you start charging $20 for Kirito except he can fly (he always could by spamming one attack anyway), and has only one unique skill and one new ult. Everything else was copy paste, even the legendary effects. And now you do Undine Asuna who doesnt even have a unique ult since its just Yuuki's with no extra animation or effort, and then only 1 new skill, also $20

Legit either make the game free and start releasing new paid characters every 2 months, or at least start giving us actual new maps, bosses, and characters. Wheres Eydis? You gave us her swimsuit and the background used for one of her units in SAOB. And she had the highest rank for gameverse character to come to the game. What about Gabe? Bercouli? Fanatio? Kizmel? Seven? Kureha? Kureha Itszuki and Zeliska should have just been guaranteed since its the same dev team that did FB. Hell, add an FB boss like the final boss or final DLC boss. The game already copied most of the assets from FB anyway. 

This game is THE DEFINITION of a perfect idea with horrible execution. Tho I am glad they are working on Offline Mode so the game can stay alive in the future when we all move on to the next title or gacha

Qué vergüenza lo que están haciendo con este juego. Ya pasó con el primero: no le dieron soporte y en menos de dos años lo abandonaron, y ahora parece que van por el mismo camino. Los fanáticos no queremos cosas inventadas ni agregados que no tienen nada que ver con el anime o el manga; queremos un juego fiel, oficial y basado en la historia original. Estuve viendo lo que están mostrando y hay cosas que no tienen sentido, como habilidades o agregados que jamás existieron, y eso arruina la experiencia para quienes seguimos la obra. Ojalá no repitan lo mismo de siempre, sacar el juego, llenarse los bolsillos y después dejarlo abandonado. Si van a hacer algo, háganlo bien, respeten el material original y a los fanáticos, porque lo único que logra este tipo de decisiones es decepcionar a la gente que realmente apoya la franquicia.

It’s a shame what they’re doing with this game. It already happened with the first one: they didn’t give it the support it deserved and in less than two years they abandoned it, leaving players and fans completely behind. And now everything suggests they’re going down the same path again.
Those of us who follow the series don’t want invented content or additions that have nothing to do with the anime or the manga; we want a faithful, official game based on the original story, respecting the material that made the franchise great. I’ve been watching what they’re showing, and there are abilities, mechanics, and additions that never existed, and that ends up ruining the experience for people who truly know and value the work.
I hope they don’t repeat the same old story: release the game, fill their pockets, and then abandon it without caring about the community. If they’re going to do something, do it right—respect the original material and the fans, because decisions like these only end up disappointing the people who genuinely support the franchise and expect a product that lives up to it.

Despite everyones harsh criticisms, I’ve always loved the Ones Justice games. In a market of sub-par area fighters trying to cash in on a popular franchise. The team over at Byking not only tried to bring a different experience than most area fighters but a passion project from people wanted to give the HeroAca series (pun not intended… kinda) Justice, with something of a quality product. Even taking surveys, and listening to criticism to keep impoving each entry. I have 100’s of hours logged into both 1 & 2. So many memories made with friends just playing multiplayer 1-1 in highschool.

If this is the last game that comes from this team. I’d like to say thank you to everyone from Byking who went above and beyond to genuinely try their best to bring a good trilogy of games to us. In all sense of the words, with what they were given, went beyond. Plus Ultra
I got a little teary eyed watching this. We've really come a long way since One's Justice.

i'll admit, not as good as the first one, given the stories are mostly disconnected, instead of a single narrative with the three DLCs wrapped around them. It also leaves a much more obvious dangling plot thread, which DLC1 was more subtle about.

That said, if there IS more to come, I look forward to it. This was still fun stuff, and it did actually do some neat things with the ideas. The Free stuff was also of high quality, with the 20 Round Battle Rush 2 really pushing you a lot, as the first was 5 mook fights and 5 boss fights. This one is STILL 5 mook fights, but everything else is bosses, and they do not pull punches.

Overall, a fine game, and I would love to see more content, paid or not, for it, as has been a tradition for the last few major entries in the series. The plot was finished overall with the main story, but it's a world left still in a bit of flux, and DLC1 and 2 both show off that not everything is stable outside of the A. Advent.

尊敬的 Bandai：
首先，恭喜你们始终如一地坚持着如此“稳定”的平衡理念。
在拥有如此庞大且多样化的角色阵容情况下，依然能够让只有极少数角色在竞技环境中真正具备实用价值——而这些角色往往“恰好”是 EX 单位——这种一致性确实令人印象深刻。
至于对回复型角色（Regen）的强化，更是令人钦佩。
或许从长远来看，你们的愿景是将这款游戏打造成一款“耐久回复模拟器”，而非真正讲求策略与多样性的对战体验。这样的方向，确实大胆。
当然，如果每 1–2 个月对 5–10 个普通角色进行调整与加强，或许能带来更多阵容多样性、玩家尝试空间以及长期活跃度。
但显然，这种投入也许并非优先事项。
毕竟，与其冒险打造一个健康的环境，不如维持一个可预测的现状。
玩家持续投入时间与资源培养非 EX 角色。
能够看到这些投入被如此“稳定地”对待，也算是一种安心。
期待下一次同样精心设计、并且精准商业化的平衡更新。
此致
敬礼

I have a lot of problems with the creator that have somewhat killed a bit of my hype. I’m still excited and managed to create a fun character but it’s very clear. A lot was sacrificed in exchange for the “freedom” in customisation. 

I love mixing and matching outfit parts. It’s very cool. Same with the mask types. So glad we can colour them. (I’m not entirely sure why we still need the masks tho. Since there’s no Miasma. Unless whatever replaced the Revenants in the new game are vulnerable to something new?)

Unfortunately…
The faces are all very similar. And the eyes just feel…smaller. The detailing in the eyes also feels less polished and the colours feel. Little muted. So many just come out white. Especially on the nails. 

I’m upset we can’t mix and match the gloves anymore but that’s more of a personal nitpick. I can get over that. 


The worst offender is by far the hair though. Any hair you add in the accessories is completely static. It has no physics like in the first game. So there is no way to attach it to the character without it looking awful. If you try to angle it at all it just looks like a stick.  

I’ve tried making a few of my code vein 1 characters and none of them are even remotely possible due to these issues 

I’m sure the full gameplay will be awesome. But the character creators, in my opinion, is somewhat below that of the original in some areas.

If this matters to you, then vote accordingly with your wallet and let Bandai Namco know how you feel about the censorship in the sequel. Even if you think this is a minor change, you should consider that there could be more censorship waiting in the full game that we are currently not privy to, so if you want to take your chances, good luck with that. I know I'll just be going back to playing Code Vein 1 after learning about the censorship. Let the cards fall where they may...

PSA: (TLDR): Code Vein 2 is censored. They censored any outfits for the player character that display underwear.

As of the release of the demo for Code Vein 2, if you compare the returning outfits in Code Vein 2 to Code Vein 1, you'll notice they added shorts to any outfit that previously showed the character's underwear. If this matters to you, then vote accordingly with your wallet and let Bandai Namco know how you feel about the censorship in the sequel. Even if you think this is a minor change, you should consider that there could be more censorship waiting in the full game that we are currently not privy to, so if you want to take your chances, good luck with that. I know I'll just be going back to playing Code Vein 1 after learning about the censorship. Let the cards fall where they may...

This is the first game in the series for PlayStation Portable (PSP). However, it is not the first game in the series for mobile platforms. In 2005, Ace Combat Advance debuted on Game Boy Advance, and Java Ace Combat: Northern Wings in 2011. Both games are not particularly well known and mix up the canon of the series and lack licensed machines. It is unknown whether the war of 2032 is part of the canon of conflicts in the Strangereal world.

The action of Ace Combat X: Skies of Deception (and its prequel Ace Combat Xi: Skies of Incursion on iOS) takes place in the southern part of Osea. For years, the Federal Republic of Aurelia lived in peace thanks to its rich resources. After the civil war in the neighboring Democratic Republic of Leasath, which lasted from 2009 to 2019, the country's leader, Diego Gaspar Navarro, grew in power and became significantly wealthier thanks to the war. After the war, poverty reigned in the country. A single glass of wine costs as much as many years of average earnings. Under the pretext of revenge for “ingratitude,” Leasath attacked Aurelia in the second half of 2020. In reality, Navarro wanted to further increase his wealth and promote the arms industry he ran. Although initially the experimental Falco squadron, led by Falco 1 and XFA -24A Apalis at its head, managed to destroy the Gandr air command cruiser, the twin Glepnir, using optical camouflage and shock wave missiles (SWBM), destroyed a significant part of Aurelia's forces, including its fleet. The armed forces had to retreat. In 10 days, 95% of Aurelia was occupied, including the capital, Griswall. The last point of resistance was the air base on Cape Aubrey. The Gryphus squadron, which usually performed naval combat missions, defended the base but was nearly destroyed by SWBM missiles. Gryphus 1 and his F-4E at the forefront began to liberate their homeland step by step. The turning point was the destruction of Glepnira over Santa Alva using the XFA-24A Apalis, the recapture of Griswall, and finally the destruction of the XFA-33A Fernir factory and the Archeleon fortress, which was shown during a broadcast in the Leasath capital, Arendal. This was the final nail in the coffin of Navarro's rule.  

The game allowed players to choose their own path through the game.

Among the machines, in addition to licensed machines, there were quite a few fictional machines. XFA-24A Apalis (similar in capabilities to the Dassault Rafale), YR-302 Fregata, XR-45 Cariburn, YR-99 Forneus, XFA-33A Fernir, as well as X-02 Wyvern and the XFA-27, returning after almost a decade. 

The flagship fighter of the game is the Lockheed Martin F-22A Raptor (designated Gryphus 1: AB 207), and in the French version, the Dassault Rafale M.

20 years of Ace Combat X: Skies of Deception in 2026 and 30 years of the Ace Combat series in 2025.

PSA: (TLDR): Code Vein 2 is censored. They censored any outfits for the player character that display underwear.

As of the release of the demo for Code Vein 2, if you compare the returning outfits in Code Vein 2 to Code Vein 1, you'll notice they added shorts to any outfit that previously showed the character's underwear. If this matters to you, then vote accordingly with your wallet and let Bandai Namco know how you feel about the censorship in the sequel. Even if you think this is a minor change, you should consider that there could be more censorship waiting in the full game that we are currently not privy to, so if you want to take your chances, good luck with that. He forgot to mention a few facts.
How the game is abandoned and forgotten by its developers.

Hackers have taken over the game for a long time. I'm talking exclusively about PVP.

Whether you're the Invader or the Host, you always encounter hackers.
They do everything to destroy any fun you wanted to have in the game.
They have all the stats maxed out, infinite HP, revive after dying right in front of you, massive damage, lag, and our attacks don't hit them, they don't take damage, they teleport, and I'm talking about people who play at level 160.
They use programs to alter the game, duplicate items, gain runes.
Not to mention those who damage your save, forcing you to delete your character and start from scratch again.
I love Elden Ring, I have almost 5,000 hours of gameplay, and I hope that in this new year the game can improve, that the developers care and take care of the game.

 I also wish for more content for the game, like new DLC, because I love this game so much and would like to play it for many more hours.

Dear Bandai,
First of all, congratulations on maintaining such a remarkably consistent balance philosophy.
It’s genuinely impressive how, despite having a large and diverse roster of characters, only a very select few are allowed to be competitively relevant — and, coincidentally, they often happen to be EX units.
The commitment to this pattern is admirable.
The regen buffs are especially inspiring.
At this point, one might assume the long-term vision is to transform the game into a healing endurance simulator rather than a strategic combat experience. Bold direction.
Of course, balancing 5–10 regular characters every 1–2 months would likely encourage roster diversity, player experimentation, and long-term engagement.
But clearly, that level of attention might be… unnecessary.
After all, why risk a healthy meta when you can preserve a predictable one?
Players continue to invest time and resources into non-EX units.
It’s reassuring to know those investments are treated with such… consistency.
Truly, the dedication to maintaining the same power hierarchy month after month is something to behold.
Looking forward to the next carefully monetized balance update.
Warm regards.

Yo Bandai, what the hell are you even doing?
Your balancing team must be asleep or completely clueless.
You keep over-buffing regen characters like you’re trying to turn the whole game into a brainless healing simulator.
There are dozens of characters in the roster, but only a tiny handful are actually viable.
And surprise, surprise — they’re mostly EX units.
So what is this? A game or just a walking cash grab?
You act like normal characters don’t exist.
Players invest time, grind resources, build units — and you just leave them useless for months.
Every month or two you could easily buff 5–10 regular characters.
But no. You drip-feed balance patches like it physically hurts you to support the full roster.
Stop pretending this is “strategy.”
It’s lazy design and greedy priorities.
If you actually cared about the player base, you’d balance the damn game instead of worshipping EX banners like they’re your only source of oxygen.
Do better.
Because right now, it just looks embarrassing

For me, games, just like every other medium of art, revolve around storytelling. There's always a story being crafted in the mind of the person who's creating something. It can be simple like Mario, or it can be deep and complex like Metal Gear Solid. Human beings are storytellers. We've been doing it for millions of years. From sculptures to literature to paintings to plays to music to movies, we love telling stories. Video games are just another way to tell those stories. Since video games are the most interactive medium, focusing on good gameplay should be paired in tandem with good storytelling. That way, you'll create a game that will transcend generations and last forever.

1:34 yo would you think that I was actually watching through all of these Bandai Namco videos, just to stop because I was busy doing errands and then I come back home didn’t have my tablet with me and just decided to watch some anime, and it ends up being the first time that I actually wanna watch this one anime called rent a girlfriend, who stars one Kazuya Kinoshima, who I end up finding out is voiced by this guy, Mr. Le at first when I heard the name. I’m like the only Kazuya that I’ve ever heard in my life, even though weirdly enough it’s a Japanese name I would’ve assumed it would’ve been popular, but the only cause I yell that I’ve heard is the Kazuya from Tekken, which is a Bandai Namco  game. So you would believe my surprise when I come back to finish up watching all of the videos from this channel and load and behold I find the voice actor for Kazuya from that anime in this video, I do want to let people know that for anybody who’s a fan of his, unfortunately he only voices the main character for seasons, one through three, unfortunately from season four onwards it’s voiced by another Alex, who is a smidge not as good as him, but at least for me it doesn’t really make that much of a difference I think the only time you really hear difference is when he’s trying to scream and yell which if you know you know, but other than that, you don’t really tell the difference unless if you just have a good ear for voice actors, and stuff like that. And whatever you do do not go to the Reddit cause whenever you bring up the voice actors for the dub they start saying that the new actors at least only for the two main characters, which is the guy that I’m talking about and the other main character of the story, Chizuku, those are the only two people that change their voice actors. Her being voice acted by Lizzy Freeman, who got changed into another voice actress by the name of I think her name is Luna Celeste, but other than those two all the other voice actors from seasons 123 come back, so if anybody wants to watch that anime, it’s really good and for any man who looks illegal? This looks girly bro trust me this anime was made for men, sure one of the main characters is on the cover of the manga and of the anime, may make you think that this is a female centric anime, and while sometimes it is kind of isn’t, this really does feel like a male anime for two reasons number one the writer is a guy which I’m kind of shocked as I never thought a guy could draw that I guess girly/feminine whatever you wanna call it, cause I don’t know I felt like I’ve seen that normally that one in anime is done by a woman you can kind of tell how the drawing from a man or a woman when it comes to anime I feel like people can kinda tell that, that’s not to say that men who create manga/anime can’t draw good looking women they can it’s just I honestly don’t know how to say this, but I just feel like the way these female characters are drawn would make you think that this is an overly feminine shojo like show/anime, but I watched it and it’s really not. It’s really good for any man that wants to watch it and as a man myself, I recommend it… It’s not finished yet because it’s going through season five I have no idea if there’s more seasons after this I’m kind of getting the feeling that this season is the last, not kid you know I literally just started watching it like two days ago. I’m kind of already caught up, but it’s really good for any man who wants to watch and is looking at. It’s like I don’t know if this is for me it’s a little bit too girly bro. I’m telling you right now it was made for men, that’s not to see that women can enjoy it. Pretty sure probably lesbian women will enjoy the show but other than that for any man who looks at that show is like bro this is not for me. I’m telling you right now you will enjoy it. I also think outside of enjoying it. I feel like there’s a lot of philosophical-esque stuff that you guys will really like in the show, and they’ll make you think really hard about relationships and just having a girlfriend in general and the rules responsibilities in the life that revolves around that it’s very interesting… So I recommend it but yeah, just going back to this voice actor load and behold when I come back to continue watching the videos and I see this voice actor just in this video for a video game. I’m like hey I know that guy he used to be the voice for Kazuya from that girlfriend anime that I was watching… For only the first three seasons… Who would’ve thought…

❌ Open world + roguelike
Open worlds depend on memory and accumulation
Roguelikes depend on reset and impermanence
Combining them cancels both:
exploration feels pointless
resets feel cruel instead of motivating
❌ Timed pressure vs learning
Souls combat requires:
observation
pattern recognition
calm repetition
Timers force:
panic
rushing
sloppy decision-making
Those philosophies conflict directly.
❌ Randomness vs mastery
Souls mastery is deterministic
Nightreign introduces:
random drops
run-dependent builds
uneven difficulty spikes
That undermines the entire skill language of the franchise.
❌ Progress feedback is broken
You were improving, but the game:
refused to show it
delayed reinforcement for dozens of hours
That’s not “hard” — that’s opaque.
Good difficulty teaches.
Nightreign withholds.

Nightreign is extremely frustrating and badly designed, especially for solo players. The bosses themselves are learnable, but the game punishes you for every mistake:

Total resets: Die once and you start from Night 1, Level 1, with 0 items, 0 revives, and basic flasks. No sense of progression or reward.

Timer pressure: You’re forced to rush for runes, healing, and upgrades, making it impossible to calmly learn bosses.

Multi-enemy bosses: Almost all bosses summon adds or become duos, punishing solo players.

Broken stagger system: Staggered bosses often block critical hits with their geometry, so melee players can’t finish combos.

Bad hitboxes and camera issues: Especially on dragons and huge enemies — lock-on often fails.

Nightlord AoEs: Massive arena-wide attacks that are hard to read and almost impossible to dodge.

Resource starvation: You constantly need flasks, revives, and stones, and mistakes leave you nearly helpless.

RNG dependence: Progress depends heavily on luck — right bosses, good drops, good runs.


The game looks amazing — the bosses and arenas are visually stunning — but the punishment system completely destroys the enjoyment. Even after defeating a Nightlord, you start again from zero. The design actively punishes mastery instead of rewarding it, making it one of the worst FromSoftware-inspired games I’ve ever played.
